title: Seal rings comprising chromium and boron cast iron
abstract: A seal ring (100) comprising a chromium and boron containing cast iron alloy composition is disclosed. The cast iron alloy composition comprises each of boron, chromium and silicon in the following amounts: boron up to 1.5 wt. %; chromium from 8 to 14 wt. %; and silicon up to 3.0 wt. %. The seal ring (100) may be produced by melting a cast iron composition further comprising the foregoing alloying elements; pouring the melted alloy into a mold; cooling the melted alloy to form a cast iron seal ring; and separating the cast iron seal ring from the mold. The seal ring is typically used in the undercarriage of earth-working machines, such as in the drive train or power train of such machines.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A seal ring (100), comprising a body having a cast iron composition comprising B, Cr, and Si in the following amounts: B: up to 1.5 wt. %; Cr: 8 to 14 wt. %; and Si: up to 3 wt. %. 2. The seal ring (100) of claim 1, wherein B is present in an amount ranging from 0.5 to 1.0 wt. %, Cr is present in an amount ranging from 10 to 14 wt. %, Si is present in an amount ranging from 1.5 to 2.4 wt. %. 3. The seal ring (100) of claim 1, wherein the cast composition further includes: C: 2.8 to 3.6 wt. %; Mn: 0.40 to 1.0 wt. %; Ni: 3.0 to 5.0 wt. %; V: up to 1.0 wt. %; Mo: up to 0.80 wt. %; P: up to 0.08 wt. %; S: up to 0.2 wt. %; and the balance comprising Fe and incidental impurities. 4. The seal ring (100) of claim 1 , wherein the cast iron composition has a microstructure comprising less than 50 wt. % carbide and more than 50 wt. % of martensite, austenite and combinations thereof. 5. The seal ring (100) of claim 1, wherein: the body is generally cylindrical (140) and extends along a longitudinal axis between a load end (145) and a seal end (150); and the seal ring (100) further includes the seal flange (105) disposed at the seal end (150) of the body, the seal flange (105) circumscribing the body (140) and projecting radially from the body to a distal perimeter of the seal flange (105), the seal flange (105) including a sealing face (110) that is annular and disposed adjacent the distal perimeter (120). 6. A method of making a cast iron seal ring (100), comprising: melting an alloy composition comprising each of B, Cr, and Si in the following amounts: B: up to 1.5 wt. %; Cr: 8 to 14 wt. %; and Si: up to 3 wt. %; pouring the melted alloy into a mold; cooling the melted alloy to form a body; separating the body from the mold; and machining the body to at least one predetermined tolerance. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising heat treating the body to change at least one physical or mechanical property of the alloy. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein heat treating comprises an annealing step, a tempering step, or both and annealing and tempering step. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein said annealing step comprises heating the body to a temperature ranging from 700 to 800 Â°C for a time ranging from 1 to 3 hours, followed by cooling to less than 200 Â°C at a rate ranging from 25 to 35 Â°C per hour; and said tempering step comprises heating at a temperature from 200 to 250 Â°C for a time ranging from 1 to 3 hours. 10. The method of claim 6, wherein said pouring occurs at a temperature ranging from 1370 Â°C to 1482 Â°C.
